name: Half Moon Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C). Butter two baking sheets and set aside.
2. Make the cookies: In a large mixing bowl, combine the sugar and butter and mix until fully incorporated. Add the eggs, milk, vanilla, and lemon extract and mix until smooth.
3. In a medium bowl, combine the flours, baking powder, and salt and stir until mixed. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet mixture in batches, stirring well to combine. Using a spoon, drop spoonfuls of the dough 2 inches apart on the prepared baking sheets. Bake for 20-30 minutes or until the edges begin to brown. Allow to cool completely.
4. Make the frosting: Place the confectioner's sugar in a large bowl. Gradually add boiling water to the sugar, stirring constantly, until the mixture is thick and spreadable.
5. Remove half of the frosting and place it on top of a double boiler set over simmering water. Add the chocolate and warm the mixture, stirring, until the chocolate is melted and the frosting is smooth. Remove from heat. Using a brush, coat one half of the flat side of the cookies with chocolate frosting and the other half with white frosting. These are puffy cake-like cookies.
